u_color_scheme_rules_5_0006.text: - **Background** → the gradient PNG, applied as the section background, sized `cover`, `no-repeat`, centered, spanning the full content width (600px). Always set a solid `background-color` of Sunshine `#faa31b` as fallback, since clients like Outlook drop background images.

u_color_scheme_rules_5_0007.text: - **Headline** → Brand Blue `#00529b`, centered, sitting in the **top third**, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~28px, one line.

u_color_scheme_rules_5_0008.text:
- **Button** → centered, in the **lower-middle** below the headline. Fill Deep Blue `#003D74`, white label (Nunito Sans 800, 18px), with a Gold `#FFC20E` arrow `›` (24px) after two non-breaking spaces. Size 248 × 64px.
**Background image:** asset is `https://solstice-public-forever.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/design_library/12912e15-c371-4bf0-a5b9-08a7f8c57d20/7772aba5-1ff5-4985-80bf-c15b782111a4/Gradient_callout.png` — do not re-host or recolor. The background may be skipped **only in very, very rare cases** (plain-text/AMP fallback, a client that strips all images, a hard size budget). When skipped, the section still renders on a solid Sunshine `#faa31b` fill and every other rule stays identical.

u_color_scheme_rules_5_0010.text: **Button radius (mandatory, never change):** `border-radius: 49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px;` — this four-value asymmetric radius is the signature shape and is **always required on every instance**, even when the background is skipped.

u_color_scheme_rules_5_0011.text: Never simplify it to a single value, swap the corners, or round the decimals.

u_color_scheme_rules_5_0015.text: ```xml Talk to your doctor about how LISRAYA can help Visit LISRAYA.com  ❯ ```

u_color_scheme_rules_5_0017.text: If the background must be skipped, remove only `background-url`, `background-size`, `background-repeat`, and `background-position` — keep `background-color="#faa31b"` and the headline color, button color, and button radius exactly as above.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0002.text: A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge** � the edge runs through the badge's center so it reads as half-on / half-off the box.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0003.text: Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0007.text: - **Container** ? the callout sits on the page/section background (usually White `#ffffff`). The blue box itself is **inset from the left by half the badge width** so the badge's outer half has room � this is the "box gets a little smaller on the left" step. Everything else stays full content width.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0008.text: - **Box** ? Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape. Vertically sized to its text with generous padding; the **left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap** (? `W/2 + 24px`).

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0009.text: - **Text** ? Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box. Never let copy slide under the badge.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0010.text: - **Icon badge** ? a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke), **vertically centered** on the box and **horizontally centered on the box's left edge** (the edge is the badge's perpendicular bisector ? 50% over the blue, 50% over the page background).

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0015.text: - Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge** (badge `left:0` inside a `padding-left:40px` wrapper ? badge center `40px` = box edge). Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0016.text: - Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height).

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0018.text: **Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order � large on **top-left + bottom-right**, near-point on **top-right + bottom-left**, a **5:1 ratio**.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0019.text: The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR).

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0020.text: Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0022.text: **Drop shadow (mandatory):** the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box � e.g. `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity).

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0023.text: This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0029.text: | Brand Blue | `#00529b` | Headline text + icon stroke |

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0036.text:
```xml
These improvements occurred as early as four weeks after starting treatment, and they continued getting better through the end of the 52-week study.
```

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0040.text: The 50/50 overhang relies on `position:absolute`, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores** � there the badge drops to the top-left in normal flow and the box won't be inset.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0041.text: For pixel-perfect fidelity across **all** clients, the brand-safe option is to **pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG** and place it via the same absolute positioning, or bake the entire badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0042.text: When that's not feasible, ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) � every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

u_color_scheme_rules_6_0047.text: If your real badge is a different size, keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`** � and the bisector stays exact.

u_content_pattern_rules_0_0001.text: - Always write **LISRAYA** in ALL CAPS in all written materials (patient marketing rule; supersedes the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule).

u_content_pattern_rules_0_0002.text: - Include the **® registered trademark symbol only when the generic name is included** — i.e., LISRAYA® (brepocitinib). Apply on **first/most prominent mention only**, in **external-facing documents only**. Do not repeat ® on subsequent mentions.

u_content_pattern_rules_0_0003.text: - Generic name format: lowercase, in parentheses: `(brepocitinib)`.

u_content_pattern_rules_0_0004.text: - Parent company: Priovant Therapeutics, Inc. (sign-off lives in the locked footer — do not add it in sections).

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0001.text: | Level | Print | Digital / Email | Word / PPT / Excel fallback |

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0003.text: | Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos** |

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0005.text: | Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos |

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0007.text: - Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0008.text: - Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0010.text: - **[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`. Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0012.text: - H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0014.text: - H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0015.text: - Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0016.text: - Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0017.text: - Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0018.text: - Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0019.text: - **Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

u_content_pattern_rules_1_0020.text: - Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

u_content_pattern_rules_2_0001.text: - **Headlines/headings:** Initial caps (title case) — capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions. Example: *LISRAYA Targets the Root Cause of Dermatomyositis*.

u_content_pattern_rules_2_0002.text: - **Campaign headline "YOU DESERVE MORE": ALL CAPS, always** (see 1.6).

u_content_pattern_rules_2_0003.text: - **Chart/infographic headlines: ALL CAPS**, set in Agenda (print) / Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback).

u_content_pattern_rules_2_0005.text: - **[GENERAL]** Do not use ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheads other than the cases above.

u_content_pattern_rules_3_0000.text: ### Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**

heading_path: Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
u_content_pattern_rules_3_0001.text: - Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.

heading_path: Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
u_content_pattern_rules_3_0002.text: - Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).

heading_path: Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
u_content_pattern_rules_3_0003.text: - Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.

heading_path: Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
u_content_pattern_rules_3_0004.text: - Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

heading_path: Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
u_content_pattern_rules_3_0005.text: - No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

u_content_pattern_rules_4_0001.text: - **Abbreviation "DM":** After first mention of "dermatomyositis," use "DM" in running copy to avoid repetition. **Do NOT use "DM" in headlines, headings, or subheads** — always the full word there (clarity, accessibility, and SEO on web). First-mention pattern: `dermatomyositis (DM)`.

u_content_pattern_rules_4_0002.text: - Because sections are assembled later: **[GENERAL] every standalone section must establish `dermatomyositis (DM)` at its own first mention** unless the assembly brief states the abbreviation is established upstream.

u_content_pattern_rules_4_0003.text: - **Inclusive language (patient materials):** say "someone with dermatomyositis" / "people with dermatomyositis" — **not "patients."** ("Patients" is acceptable only in HCP materials.) Use **"care team"** (not "staff") for the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems.

u_content_pattern_rules_4_0004.text: - **Numbers:** spell out zero–nine; numerals for 10+. **Exceptions — always numerals: percentages, ages, ratios.** Percentages use the % sign (e.g., 74%).

u_content_pattern_rules_4_0005.text: - **Data tone:** when citing clinical data, avoid negative or fear-based language; use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying. Example pattern: "Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo."

u_content_pattern_rules_4_0006.text: - **Punctuation:** Oxford/serial comma always in series of 3+.

u_content_pattern_rules_5_0001.text: - Set in **Agenda** (digital may use Agenda as live text for this headline only; email fallback: Nunito Sans ExtraBold).

u_content_pattern_rules_5_0003.text: - The letters **"D" (in DESERVE) and "M" (in MORE) are set at approximately 40% tint** of the headline color — a nod to the "d" and "m" of dermatomyositis.

u_content_pattern_rules_5_0004.text: - Two approved colorways: brand **Gold/Sunshine** or **Brand Blue** (tinted letters at 40% of the same hue).

u_content_pattern_rules_5_0005.text: - Stack as three lines (YOU / DESERVE / MORE) in hero placements, left-aligned.

u_content_pattern_rules_5_0006.text: - Full lockup copy: "**YOU DESERVE MORE.** So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis."

u_content_pattern_rules_6_0000.text: ### Callout Formatting (three approved styles — use judiciously; never two of the same style competing on one screen/section)

heading_path: Callout Formatting (three approved styles — use judiciously; never two of the same style competing on one screen/section)
u_content_pattern_rules_6_0001.text: 1. **Main callout (key point):** Bold **Brand Blue (#00529b)** text with **Gold (#ffc60a)** horizontal rules above and below. Centered text. [GENERAL: rule weight 2px, rule width ≈ 60–100% of text block, 16px padding between rule and text.]

heading_path: Callout Formatting (three approved styles — use judiciously; never two of the same style competing on one screen/section)
u_content_pattern_rules_6_0002.text: 2. **Secondary/data callout:** **Light Blue (#E6F0F9) box at 70% tint** with **Bold Brand Blue** text; may include one line icon (e.g., 1x pill icon). [GENERAL: 20px internal padding, corner radius per accent-shape rules §2.5.]

heading_path: Callout Formatting (three approved styles — use judiciously; never two of the same style competing on one screen/section)
u_content_pattern_rules_6_0003.text: 3. **Text-heavy callout (lists/descriptions):** **Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) box at 30% tint**, **Bold Brand Blue headline**, line icons, **Bold Sky Blue (#358CCB) subheads**, **Regular Graphite (#212121) body text**. [GENERAL: 24px internal padding; two-column icon+text layout collapses to single column on mobile.]

u_content_pattern_rules_7_0001.text: - **Headline:** YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

u_content_pattern_rules_7_0002.text: - **Product benefits:** "LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks the specific inflammatory signals that drive dermatomyositis, so you can:" → bullets: *Increase your muscle strength* / *Improve your skin symptoms* / *Rely less on steroids* / *Do more of the activities you love*.

u_content_pattern_rules_7_0003.text: - **MOA (primary):** "Unlike other treatments for dermatomyositis (DM), which suppress the immune system generally, LISRAYA targets the root cause of the disease."

u_content_pattern_rules_7_0004.text: - **MOA (alt):** "...LISRAYA works by turning off the specific inflammatory switches that are stuck in the 'on' position."

u_content_pattern_rules_7_0005.text: - **Administration:** "LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required."

u_content_pattern_rules_7_0006.text: - Doctor-discussion CTA pattern: "Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for."

u_content_pattern_rules_7_0007.text: - Support program name: **My Compass Support** (patient support: Personalized Communication via PAL — text, phone, email, in person; Authorization Support).

u_content_pattern_rules_8_0001.text: - **Confident, not boastful** — first-of-its-kind significance stated clearly and directly, never overstated or sensationalized.

u_content_pattern_rules_8_0002.text: - **Warm, not overly emotional** — genuine empathy about DM's daily impact; patients at the center.

u_content_pattern_rules_8_0003.text: - **Clear, not complicated** — plain, respectful language; easy to understand without losing accuracy.

u_content_pattern_rules_8_0004.text: - Tone may flex slightly by context; core voice never changes.

u_content_pattern_rules_9_0001.text: - Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).

u_content_pattern_rules_9_0002.text: - Patient quotes: attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.

u_content_pattern_rules_9_0003.text: - Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**

u_design_pattern_rules_0_0000.text: ### Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0001.text: - Email body width: **600px** fixed, centered; sections must render edge-to-edge at exactly 600px so assembled sections butt cleanly.

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0002.text: - Mobile breakpoint: **480px**; all multi-column layouts collapse to single column.

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0003.text: - Internal section grid: 12-column mental model; practical layouts are 1-col, 2-col (50/50), or image/text split (≈40/60).

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0004.text: - **Section side padding: 24px** left/right (consistent across ALL sections — this is what makes assembly seamless). Full-bleed imagery/gradient backgrounds may run to 600px, but text within them still respects 24px insets.

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0005.text: - **Vertical rhythm: 8px base unit.** All padding/margins are multiples of 8.

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0006.text: - Section top/bottom internal padding: **32px** (each section owns its own top AND bottom padding; no external margins between sections — assembly is zero-gap stacking).

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0007.text: - Heading → body gap: 16px. Paragraph → paragraph: 16px. Body → callout/CTA: 24px. Image → caption: 8px.

heading_path: Email Canvas & Grid **[GENERAL — not specified in guide; Solstice email baseline]**
u_design_pattern_rules_0_0008.text: - Buttons/CTA **[GENERAL, contrast-checked to palette]:** Brand Blue #00529b background, White #ffffff text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, padding 14px 32px, **min touch target 48px height** (brand accessibility rule: ≥48px clickable areas), corner radius 24px (fully rounded pill, echoing the rounded brand aesthetic). Secondary CTA: White background, 2px Brand Blue border, Brand Blue text, same metrics. One primary CTA per section maximum.

u_design_pattern_rules_1_0001.text: - Fluid wave forms in **two colorways**: **Sunshine/Gold** and **Sky Blue/Deep Blue**; each in **long and short** variants.

u_design_pattern_rules_1_0002.text: - Construction: top wave layer at **70% transparency** over the base wave (Gold over Sunshine; Sky Blue over Deep Blue) — always preserve the layered, translucent quality.

u_design_pattern_rules_1_0003.text: - Usage: alone, or overlaid on photography or gradient backgrounds; to support layout flow, frame content, or add depth. Approved color values only.

u_design_pattern_rules_1_0004.text: - **Masthead gradient with logo mark** (Sunshine → 0C,10M,78Y,0K, logo mark placed at the lightest end) is the approved device for holding copy in masthead positions.

u_design_pattern_rules_2_0001.text: - Photography: **warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery** — real moments of simple, everyday activity (walking the dog, beach, BBQ). Conveys returning to everyday life and "getting back to themselves."

u_design_pattern_rules_2_0002.text: - Images are housed in the **Accent shape** (§2.5) — asymmetric rounded-corner crops, not plain rectangles or circles.

u_design_pattern_rules_2_0003.text: - Waves and gradients may overlay photography.

u_design_pattern_rules_2_0004.text: - Every image requires **descriptive alt text** (brand accessibility rule).

u_design_pattern_rules_3_0001.text: - A rectangle with **selectively enlarged opposite corner radii**; the other two corners keep a slight radius. Used to house callout text and photography. Reads modern and optimistic.

u_design_pattern_rules_3_0002.text: - Approved radius pairs (print): **1.25" & 0.0625"** (large containers/photos), **0.5" & 0.0625"** (banners), **0.4375" & 0.0625"** (small callout boxes).

u_design_pattern_rules_3_0003.text: - **[GENERAL] Email px conversions (at 96dpi):** large: 120px & 6px; banner: 48px & 6px; callout: 42px & 6px. Apply the large radius to one diagonal pair (e.g., top-left + bottom-right), the 6px radius to the other pair. Be consistent within an email: same diagonal orientation across all sections (default: large radius top-left & bottom-right).

u_design_pattern_rules_3_0004.text: - **Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners** (not the asymmetric accent shape). [GENERAL: 12px uniform radius.]

u_design_pattern_rules_4_0001.text: - Clean, easy to scan, consistent with the system. Container: uniform rounded corners, Light Blue (#E6F0F9) panel with a Brand Blue rounded header bar.

u_design_pattern_rules_4_0002.text: - **Color logic: Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) dominant; Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) reserved for emphasis on key figures/data points only.** Placebo/comparator: neutral gray.

u_design_pattern_rules_4_0003.text: - Bars and chart icons: **rounded, approachable** shapes (rounded bar tops).

u_design_pattern_rules_4_0004.text: - Headline: Agenda (print) / Nunito Sans Bold+ (email), **ALL CAPS**, Brand Blue, with a thin Gold rule beneath.

u_design_pattern_rules_4_0005.text: - Statistical callouts: **large and bold** (the % figure is the hero), supporting copy in a **lighter weight beneath**.

u_design_pattern_rules_4_0006.text: - Footnotes: noticeably smaller, clearly separated from primary data.

u_design_pattern_rules_5_0001.text: - Style: **minimal line illustration** for clarity and quick recall. New icons are permitted but **must match this stylistic approach** (consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color line work).

u_design_pattern_rules_5_0002.text: - Icon colors and backgrounds adjustable **only within the brand palette**.

u_design_pattern_rules_5_0003.text: - Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. [GENERAL: minimum render size 32×32px in email; default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.]

u_design_pattern_rules_6_0001.text: - Generous white space; clean, never cluttered. **Limit focal points per section** — use contrast to direct attention to the single most important element.

u_design_pattern_rules_6_0002.text: - "Show, don't tell": break up text walls with imagery and icons.

u_design_pattern_rules_6_0003.text: - Color blocks delineate topics (My Compass webpage pattern) — alternate White / Light Blue / Golden Sand / gradient section backgrounds so adjacent assembled sections don't visually merge. **[GENERAL]: section designers must declare their section's background color in the component metadata so assemblers can sequence without two identical adjacent backgrounds.**

u_design_pattern_rules_6_0004.text: - Bold headline + simple benefit statements + patient quote is the canonical content block pattern (per patient brochure, which is the **reference template for visuals, messaging, and overall tone of ALL LISRAYA materials**).

u_other_rules_0_0002.text: - Line height **≥150%**; more for light-on-dark text.

u_other_rules_0_0003.text: - Use a type scale for visual hierarchy.

u_other_rules_0_0004.text: - Test contrast; never rely on color alone; proof all text for legibility.

u_other_rules_0_0005.text: - Forms: labels always visible (no placeholder-as-label, no disappearing labels); click/tap targets **≥48px**.

u_other_rules_0_0006.text: - Animations: quick transitions that don't interrupt flow; avoid excessive motion; provide a way to "pause" effects; design for phone, tablet, and desktop. **[GENERAL email]:** animated GIFs must carry the key message in frame 1 (Outlook shows only the first frame); no autoplaying motion longer than ~5s without a static fallback.

u_other_rules_0_0007.text: - Media: descriptive **alt text** on all images; transcripts available for any video or audio.

u_other_rules_1_0001.text: - This guide governs **patient (DTC) materials**. Language rules differ by audience: "someone/people with dermatomyositis" for patient materials; "patients" acceptable in HCP materials. Confirm audience in every brief before writing.

u_other_rules_1_0002.text: - Patient brochure = master reference for visuals, messaging, and tone across **all** LISRAYA materials.

u_other_rules_2_0000.text: ### Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**

heading_path: Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
u_other_rules_2_0001.text: - Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.

heading_path: Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
u_other_rules_2_0002.text: - Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

heading_path: Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
u_other_rules_2_0003.text: - Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

heading_path: Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
u_other_rules_2_0004.text: - Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

heading_path: Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
u_other_rules_2_0005.text: - Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

heading_path: Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
u_other_rules_2_0006.text: - All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.
